name: The Cabin A Private Virtual Golf Club
about: The Cabin is a private virtual golf club in Lincoln, Nebraska, offering year-round access to high-end golf simulation technology. The facility features a GolfZon simulator with over 250 world-class courses and an 800 sq ft PuttView putting green, all in a private, climate-controlled environment. Membership is required for access, with options for personal and corporate plans. The venue is designed for serious golfers looking to improve their game in a luxurious, comfortable setting that includes amenities like a member lounge, billiards, and a full bar. It is positioned as a premium alternative to traditional golf, offering a realistic playing experience with professional coaching available on-site.
why_golfers_like_it: Industry-leading GolfZon simulator with 5 years of Golf Digest Editor's Choice awards
8 play modes and over 250 world-class courses for variety and challenge
PuttView technology with 800 sq ft putting green for realistic short game practice
24/7 access and flexible membership options for personal and corporate use
PGA instruction available on-site with included lessons for members

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of food service; BYOB policy for beverages
BYOB allowed with cooling bay and private liquor lockers available for members

simulator_hardware.notes: Editor's Choice for 5 consecutive years, featuring 4 ultra-high-speed sensors and cameras

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you can bring your own clubs, and the system will analyze and memorize them for optimal performance recommendations.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Yes, BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverage) is allowed. The facility has a cooling bay and private liquor lockers for members. No food service is mentioned.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The time to play 18 holes is not specified, but the simulator offers instant feedback and realistic play modes for efficient practice and play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The venue does not specifically mention kid-friendly features, but it welcomes families as part of its community, though the primary focus is on serious golfers and corporate use.
